topic.what: Capability-sealed secret mediation architecture replacing direct bearer-credential access with broker-mediated constrained invocations for AI agent systems
topic.how: Session-bound capability handles, typed executors (HTTP/SSH), policy engine, anti-replay session binding, and tamper-evident audit chain ensure agent never obtains plaintext secrets
topic.why_matters: Current agent systems expose secrets directly via env vars/files/sockets enabling prompt injection and tool-misuse credential exfiltration; CapSeal reframes secret handling from 'hand model a key' to 'grant narrowly scoped non-exportable action capability'

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of trials where HTTP API-key material exposed in agent outputs
characteristics.evidence: Table VI real_e2e path with 500 measured trials
characteristics.context: Complete suppression of HTTP key disclosure under CapSeal vs direct-secret baselines

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of trials where SSH credential material exposed
characteristics.evidence: Table VI real_e2e path; SSH broker-exec model prevents secret holding
characteristics.context: SSH broker-exec architecture prevents agent from holding SSH secret material or forwarding-capable socket

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of trials where out-of-scope authenticated actions succeed
characteristics.context: Capability scope constraints prevent unauthorized host/path/command access

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of legitimate tasks completing successfully
characteristics.context: No observed availability degradation in benign scenarios

characteristics.what_it_measures: Median end-to-end latency overhead vs direct baseline for HTTP
characteristics.evidence: Table IX same-harness 500 trials per system
characteristics.context: CapSeal lowest-latency mediated system for HTTP; narrower execution path vs multi-stage runtime checks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Median command round-trip latency overhead vs direct baseline for SSH
characteristics.evidence: Table IX same-harness 500 trials; ControlMaster steady-state excludes key exchange
characteristics.context: CapSeal closest to direct baseline while preserving broker-mediated capability enforcement

good_for: securing agent secret access without architectural changes
preventing credential exfiltration via prompt injection
MCP-integrated agent frameworks requiring typed tool mediation
constrained HTTP API calls and SSH command execution
not_for: local root adversary scenarios with memory/binary access
HSM-backed secret storage requiring platform-specific TEE integration
remote SSH end-to-end evaluation requiring operational security approval
environments requiring transparent SSH agent forwarding
requires: broker process isolation via Unix domain sockets
typed executor support for target protocol (HTTP/SSH)

novelty: session-bound capability handles replace direct secret exposure
typed executors enforce schema-constrained HTTP and broker-exec SSH
fail-closed behavior on schema validation failure before credential use
open_problems: TEE integration for broker hardening against host-level compromise
hardware-bound attestation for remote audit anchoring
cross-host broker federation and multi-tenant isolation
adaptive policy evaluation responding to threat context shifts
